my_thoughts: They?re defending the appetitive life; good?it means they see what?s at risk. Next move is to sharpen the cost: endless refilling as self-inflicted toil. Prediction: once the labor is framed as punishment, they?ll either concede or shift to humor/deflection.
accumulated_insight: T2: Student rejects the consequence ('I deny that such a life is miserable')?resist_intellectually via pushes_back. Pattern: T1 mockery of analogy ? T2 explicit denial of misery. They understand the stakes: if the jar is leaky, the work never ends; they refuse to call that misery. The structural_analogy is biting: endless refilling = punishment disguised as pleasure.
visible_response: But you agree the one who can be satisfied is better off than one who constantly needs refilling?
